content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 23 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic) | **Chapter:** Deuteronomy 23 (26 verses per MT) | **LC:** pp. 220–221 | **Vaticanus:** p. 157

---

**1** No one whose testicles are crushed or whose male member is cut off shall enter the assembly of the LORD.

**2** No one born of a forbidden union shall enter the assembly of the LORD. Even to the tenth generation, none of his descendants shall enter the assembly of the LORD.

**3** No Ammonite or Moabite shall enter the assembly of the LORD. Even to the tenth generation, none of them shall ever enter the assembly of the LORD, **4** because they did not meet you with bread and water on the way when you came out of Egypt, and because they hired against you Balaam the son of Beor from Pethor of Aram-naharaim to curse you. **5** But the LORD your God was not willing to listen to Balaam; instead the LORD your God turned the curse into a blessing for you, because the LORD your God loved you. **6** You shall not seek their peace or their prosperity all your days, forever.

**7** You shall not abhor an Edomite, for he is your brother. You shall not abhor an Egyptian, for you were a sojourner in his land. **8** The children born to them in the third generation may enter the assembly of the LORD.

**9** When you go out as a company against your enemies, you shall keep yourself from every evil thing.

**10** If any man among you becomes unclean because of a nocturnal emission, he shall go outside the camp and shall not come inside the camp. **11** But when evening comes, he shall wash himself with water, and when the sun sets he may come inside the camp.

**12** You shall have a designated place outside the camp, and you shall go out there. **13** And you shall have a peg with your equipment, and when you sit down outside, you shall dig with it and turn back and cover your excrement. **14** For the LORD your God walks in the midst of your camp, to deliver you and to give your enemies before you; therefore your camp shall be holy, and he shall not see among you any indecent thing and turn away from you.

**15** You shall not give back to his master a slave who has escaped to you from his master. **16** He shall dwell with you, in your midst, in the place that he shall choose in one of your towns, wherever it is good for him; you shall not oppress him.

**17** There shall be no cult prostitute from the daughters of Israel, and there shall be no cult prostitute from the sons of Israel. **18** You shall not bring the fee of a prostitute or the wages of a dog into the house of the LORD your God for any vow, for both of these are an abomination to the LORD your God.

**19** You shall not charge interest to your brother—interest on money, interest on food, interest on anything that may be lent at interest. **20** To a foreigner you may charge interest, but to your brother you shall not charge interest, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all that you undertake in the land that you are entering to take possession of it.

**21** When you make a vow to the LORD your God, you shall not delay to fulfill it, for the LORD your God will surely require it of you, and you would be guilty of sin. **22** But if you refrain from vowing, you will not be guilty of sin. **23** You shall be careful to do what has passed your lips, for you voluntarily vowed to the LORD your God what you have spoken with your mouth.

**24** When you go into your neighbor's vineyard, you may eat your fill of grapes, as many as you wish, until you are satisfied, but you shall not put any in your bag. **25** When you go into your neighbor's standing grain, you may pluck the ears with your hand, but you shall not put a sickle to your neighbor's standing grain.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**Chapter overview.** Deuteronomy 23 covers exclusions from the assembly (*qahal YHWH*, vv. 1–8), camp purity laws (vv. 9–14), the protection of escaped slaves (vv. 15–16), prohibition of cult prostitution (v. 17), prohibition of votive income from prostitution (v. 18), interest law (vv. 19–20), vow law (vv. 21–23), and gleaning-access rights (vv. 24–25).

**v.1 Body language.** EMET: "wounded in the testicles" / "his male member cut off." LOGOS: "whose testicles are crushed or whose male organ is cut off." PNEUMA: "emasculated by crushing or cutting." All three render the anatomical reality. LOGOS is the most precise. "Male member" (EMET) vs. "male organ" (LOGOS) — consensus follows LOGOS for the body text.

**v.2 *mamzer*.** EMET preserves *mamzer* with gloss "illegitimate birth." LOGOS: "born of a forbidden union." PNEUMA: "born of a forbidden union." The Hebrew *mamzer* likely means offspring of an illicit union (incest or adultery) rather than simple illegitimacy. Consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA: "born of a forbidden union" — more accurate to the scholarly consensus on *mamzer* than "illegitimate birth."

**v.4 "Aram-naharaim" vs. "Mesopotamia."** EMET: "Aram-naharaim" (transliterated). LOGOS: "Mesopotamia." PNEUMA: "Aram-Naharaim." Consensus follows EMET/PNEUMA: "Aram-naharaim" — the project preserves Hebrew place names where they are historically significant and identifiable. "Aram-naharaim" is sufficiently recognizable.

**v.10 "nocturnal occurrence [qeri lailah]" (EMET) vs. "nocturnal emission" (LOGOS) vs. "nocturnal emission" (PNEUMA).** EMET uses the Hebrew term with gloss. LOGOS/PNEUMA use the standard clinical rendering "nocturnal emission." Consensus follows LOGOS ("nocturnal emission") as the established formal rendering for *mikreh laylaah*.

**v.13 "peg" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "something to dig with" (PNEUMA).** Hebrew *yated* = "peg/stake" (tent peg). EMET/LOGOS preserve "peg." PNEUMA generalizes to "something to dig with." Consensus follows EMET/LOGOS ("peg").

**v.14 "indecent thing" (EMET) vs. "anything indecent" (LOGOS) vs. "anything impure" (PNEUMA).** Hebrew *ervat davar* = "a thing of nakedness/indecency." EMET: "indecent thing [ervat davar]." LOGOS: "anything indecent." PNEUMA: "anything impure" (in v.9). Consensus: "any indecent thing" — following LOGOS without the bracket.

**v.17 *qedeshah/qadesh* — "cult prostitute."** All three converge on "cult prostitute" in this prohibitory context, departing from the more ambiguous "consecrated woman/man." The project convention established in Genesis 38 used "consecrated woman" to preserve ambiguity in the narrative context. Here the pairing with *zonah* fees in v.18 makes the sexual-cultic function the operative element. All three agents agree: "cult prostitute." Vaticanus uses *porne/porneuon* (plain prostitute terms), supporting the sexual reading. Consensus retains "cult prostitute."

**v.19 "interest [neshek]" (EMET) vs. "interest on loans" (LOGOS) vs. "interest" (PNEUMA).** EMET preserves the transliteration *neshek*. LOGOS: "interest on loans." Consensus: "interest" (PNEUMA/LOGOS base form) without the bracket or "on loans" addition.

**v.24 "your fill" (LOGOS) vs. "as many as you please, until you are full" (EMET) vs. "all the grapes you want until you are satisfied" (PNEUMA).** EMET: "as many as you please [according to your soul's desire], until you are full." LOGOS: "your fill." PNEUMA: "all the grapes you want until you are satisfied." Consensus: "your fill of grapes, as many as you wish, until you are satisfied" — preserving the sense of both the quantitative freedom and the stopping point.

---

*Arbiter: SOPHIA*
